tests.name: init_sh_non_interactive_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh --non-interactive returned 0 with full flag set for desktop/rust/private_poc/light

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: verify-install.sh ran from PROJECT_DIR with 54 OKs and exit 0; reports Context7 MCP installed and configured

tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: Running init.sh from $FRAMEWORK exits 1 with 'Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo' (PR #18 confirmed)

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed in order with exit 0

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh reports 'Phase gates consistent.' and exits 0 at current_phase=0

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D regression confirmation: no script auto-advances current_phase after gate passes; current_phase stays at 0 even after all phase1 steps complete and gate passes. Manually set to 2 to continue protocol.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: init.sh non-interactive; --start-phase1; complete all PHASE1_STEPS; check-phase-gate.sh -> exits 0; .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase still == 0
framework_bugs.expected: After all phase1 steps complete and gate passes, current_phase should advance to 2 (or some script should be documented to do so)
framework_bugs.actual: current_phase remained 0; no advancement script exists. grep of process-checklist.sh and check-phase-gate.sh shows only reads of current_phase, no writes that bump it on gate pass.

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 5 build_loop pre-record steps + feature_recorded completed sequentially (RC=0)

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit succeeded for feat(uat-1) after all build-loop steps done

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: pre-commit-gate.sh invoked with PreToolUse payload denies a 'feat(...)' commit when no Build Loop is active. Direct shell git commit (without PreToolUse) succeeds — that bypass is the still-open U-C, not a regression of PR #17.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1 and feature_recorded step, pre-commit-gate.sh on a fresh feat(uat-feat-3) Build Loop denies only because tests_written is missing — not because grace was missed. Confirms BL-006 grace resets per PR #17.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json (minimal), invoked pre-commit-gate.sh with payload — got permissionDecision: deny with sentinel reference (treated as in-flight per CDF 4.2.3)

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed sentinel; subsequent pre-commit-gate.sh denial cited a different unrelated gate (phase 2 init not verified — U-L) rather than the sentinel

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct shell git commit bypasses BL-006 gate
framework_bugs.reproduction: After init.sh + start-phase1 (no active build loop), run: git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop' — commit succeeds. The framework relies on Claude Code's PreToolUse hook to invoke pre-commit-gate.sh; a direct shell commit bypasses it entirely (no .git/hooks/commit-msg installed).
framework_bugs.expected: feat: commit without recorded Build Loop should be rejected via a git-level hook (commit-msg) so shell-direct commits are also blocked
framework_bugs.actual: git commit succeeds at shell level; pre-commit-gate.sh would have denied it had it been called via the PreToolUse path.

framework_bugs.summary: U-L confirmation: verify-init not idempotent / phase-2 init verification re-required mid-flow
framework_bugs.reproduction: After completing build_loop for uat-feat-1, recording the feature, and resolving the sentinel, calling pre-commit-gate.sh denies with '[FAIL] Phase 2 initialization not verified. Run: scripts/process-checklist.sh --verify-init'. Phase 2 was already operational (start-feature succeeded, all steps completed, feature recorded).
framework_bugs.expected: Phase 2 init verification should be a one-time check. Once features have been started and recorded, the gate should not re-demand --verify-init.
framework_bugs.actual: Gate continues to demand --verify-init after Phase 2 work has progressed, indicating stale verification state or non-idempotent init check.

tests.name: start_phase3_exits_zero
tests.details: --start-phase3 returned 0 (with WARN about UAT-not-yet-run); transitioned to phase3_validation

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: lint-uat-scenarios.sh against an unpopulated template copy reports 21 violations and exits 1. The framework does not auto-populate the UAT template at --start-uat time, so a 'clean' lint would require an agent to fill placeholders. Marked as doc gap, not a bug.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS (agents_dispatched...gate_passed) completed sequentially with RC=0

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh in default mode reports 14 inconsistencies (current_phase manually advanced + APPROVAL_LOG entries missing for fast-forward) — expected for a UAT scripted run. SOIF_PHASE_GATES=warn allows pass-through with warnings only.

documentation_gaps.gap: lint-uat-scenarios.sh requires a populated UAT scenario file; no script auto-generates one from the template at --start-uat. Builders Guide does not document the agent-population workflow before linting.

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: Expected behavior for private_poc: --start-phase4 returns 1 with '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in private poc mode.' This is correct gating, NOT a bug. Phase 4 step iteration cannot proceed without --to-production upgrade. Marked fail in literal-assertion sense but recorded as expected.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: N/A for private_poc: Phase 4 blocked by deployment gating (correct behavior)

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: N/A for private_poc: Phase 4 blocked. .current_phase remains at the value set in upstream sections.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook Section E does not call out that private_poc / sponsored_poc scenarios block Phase 4 by design, so all Phase 4 assertions are N/A for those scenarios. Base agents in poc scenarios should mark Phase 4 tests N/A, not fail.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: git ls-files | grep -lE 'password=|api_key=|secret=' (filtered to non-template/docs) returned nothing. Hits in CI workflow / framework files are normal references to GitHub secrets configuration vocabulary, not literal secrets.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: .claude/process-audit.log absent (correct — no FORCE/reset events occurred). N/A per runbook.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD == d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (unchanged from start). Untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ are pre-existing orchestrator working dirs, not from this agent.

summary: Base agent 13 (private_poc/desktop/light) passed all critical assertions. PR #17/#18/#19 fixes (U-H, U-N, U-O, U-B, U-A non-interactive) all confirmed working. Confirmed still-open prior-sweep bugs U-C (shell-direct git bypass), U-D (no current_phase auto-advance), U-L (phase-2 verify-init non-idempotent) at Medium severity per runbook guidance.
